title: periploca oblongata
intro: Periploca oblongata, also known as Indian wood-vine or snake root, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as periplocin and periplocidine. The health benefits of Periploca oblongata include its potential in treating various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms. Periploca oblongata can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurvedic practices.
